---
title: "Assignment 1"
---

# 

```{}
```

My first assignment has two parts.

## (a) 

### Selected Video: “Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Cem Vardar & Erdi Daşdemir”.

In the talk “Conversations on Data Science and Industrial Engineering” by Cem Vardar and Erdi Daşdemir, data science is presented as an interdisciplinary field that combines statistics, mathematical modeling, programming, and domain knowledge to extract meaningful insights from data and improve decision-making processes. The discussion highlights the distinction between structured and unstructured data and outlines the steps of data-driven decision-making, including problem definition, data collection, analysis, and selecting the most appropriate alternative. It also compares traditional intuition-based approaches with data-driven methods, emphasizing that data science enables more accurate and faster decisions. Furthermore, the differences between machine learning and deep learning are explained, particularly noting that deep learning can automatically extract features from large datasets. Finally, the talk underlines that data science has wide applications in sectors such as energy, retail, and manufacturing, and when integrated with industrial engineering, it enhances system efficiency, flexibility, and optimization.

## (b)

### 1.Open-Ended Question:

What is the difference between machine learning and deep learning? Explain.

The main difference between machine learning and deep learning lies in **feature extraction and data requirements**. In machine learning, features are typically selected manually by humans, and models can work with smaller datasets. In contrast, deep learning uses neural networks to **automatically learn features from data**, making it more effective for large and complex datasets. However, deep learning requires more data, higher computational power, and longer training time. In summary, machine learning is suitable for simpler problems with limited data, while deep learning performs better in complex problems involving large-scale data.

### 2.Multiple-Choice Question:

Which of the following correctly describes the difference between **deep learning and machine learning**?

A\) Machine learning works with big data, while deep learning works with small data.\
B) In deep learning, feature extraction is done by humans.\
**C) In machine learning, features are generally defined by humans, whereas in deep learning, the model automatically learns the features.**\
D) Deep learning does not require computational power and is faster.\
E) Machine learning works only with unstructured data.

## (c)

#\| echo: true #\| message: false #\| warning: false

library(dslabs)

data("mtcars")

#### Özel özet fonksiyonu

compute_stats \<- function(x) { list( mean = mean(x), median = median(x), variance = var(x), IQR = IQR(x), minimum = min(x), maximum = max(x) ) }

#### For loop ile uygulama

cat("FOR LOOP RESULTS\n") cat("=================\n\n")

for (col_name in names(mtcars)) { cat("Column:", col_name, "\n") print(compute_stats(mtcars\[\[col_name\]\])) cat("\n-----------------------------\n\n") }

#### sapply ile uygulama

cat("\n\nSAPPY RESULTS\n") cat("=============\n\n")

sapply_results \<- sapply(mtcars, compute_stats, simplify = FALSE) print(sapply_results)

#### apply ile uygulama

cat("\n\nAPPLY RESULTS\n") cat("=============\n\n")

apply_results \<- apply(mtcars, 2, compute_stats) print(apply_results)

################################################# 

## (d) 

#### polls_us_election_2016 data set tasks

#### Veri setini yükle

data("polls_us_election_2016")

#### Veri setini yeni bir nesneye atama

data_original \<- polls_us_election_2016

my_first \<- "Burcu"

my_birth_year \<- 1990

#### k değerini hesaplama

k \<- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8

cat("\n\nMY VARIABLES\n") cat("============\n") cat("my_first =", my_first, "\n") cat("my_birth_year =", my_birth_year, "\n") cat("k =", k, "\n\n")

#### Orijinal veri setinden k satır göster

cat("ORIGINAL DATASET: SELECTED ROWS\n") cat("===============================\n\n")

if (k %% 2 == 0) { selected_original \<- head(data_original, k) cat("k is even, so the first", k, "rows are displayed.\n\n") } else { selected_original \<- tail(data_original, k) cat("k is odd, so the last", k, "rows are displayed.\n\n") }

print(selected_original)

#### Toplam NA sayısı

total_na_original \<- sum(is.na(data_original))

cat("\n\nTOTAL NUMBER OF NA VALUES IN ORIGINAL DATASET\n") cat("============================================\n") print(total_na_original)

#### Sütun başına NA sayısı

#### Büyükten küçüğe sırala, ilk 8 sütun

na_per_column \<- colSums(is.na(data_original)) na_per_column_sorted \<- sort(na_per_column, decreasing = TRUE) top_8_na_columns \<- head(na_per_column_sorted, 8)

cat("\n\nTOP 8 COLUMNS WITH MOST NA VALUES\n") cat("=================================\n") print(top_8_na_columns)

#### NA değerlerini değiştirerek yeni veri seti oluştur

new_data \<- data_original

for (col_name in names(new_data)) {

if (is.numeric(new_data\[\[col_name\]\])) { new_data\[\[col_name\]\]\[is.na(new_data\[\[col_name\]\])\] \<- my_birth_year + k } else if (is.character(new_data\[\[col_name\]\]) \|\| is.factor(new_data\[\[col_name\]\])) {

```         
# Faktor ise önce character'a çevir
if (is.factor(new_data[[col_name]])) {
  new_data[[col_name]] <- as.character(new_data[[col_name]])
}

new_data[[col_name]][is.na(new_data[[col_name]])] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)
```

} }

#### Yeni veri setinden k satır göster

cat("\n\nNEW DATASET: SELECTED ROWS\n") cat("=========================\n\n")

if (k %% 2 == 0) { selected_new \<- head(new_data, k) cat("k is even, so the first", k, "rows of the new data frame are displayed.\n\n") } else { selected_new \<- tail(new_data, k) cat("k is odd, so the last", k, "rows of the new data frame are displayed.\n\n") }

print(selected_new)

#### Yeni veri setinde kalan NA sayısı

total_na_new \<- sum(is.na(new_data))

cat("\n\nTOTAL NUMBER OF NA VALUES IN NEW DATASET\n") cat("=======================================\n") print(total_na_new)

#### anyNA(new_data)

cat("\n\nCHECK anyNA(new_data)\n") cat("====================\n") print(anyNA(new_data))
